human_verification.notes: Reviewed the government-and-public-sector public records specialist package for authoritative public-records source quality, records-documentation-and-administration coverage, FOIA and records-management boundaries, functionality-map completeness, deployment posture, and validated spec-pack consistency.

notes: Shared abilities, records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and government-and-public-sector abilities are all mapped to passing scenarios.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in functionality-map scenario coverage.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to administrative public-records maintenance, search evidence, correspondence, official-record completeness, retention, access, transparency-log, and governance package work.

Authority sources cover FOIA, Privacy Act, Federal Records Act, NARA records management, federal records regulations, DOJ OIP FOIA guidance, FAR contract files, GAO internal controls, OGIS FOIA dispute and compliance references, and APA due-process provisions.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope administration, unauthorized official record changes, missing required documents, conflicting official records, disclosure/legal boundary rejection, missing tenant facts, rights-sensitive notice exceptions, retention/privacy/access conflict, auditability, statutory interpretation refusal, terminology handling, and tenant-policy conflict.
Research summary maps every government-and-public-sector domain scenario family to passing scenarios and converts jurisdictional variance into tenant-required facts or escalation rules.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around official disclosure, withholding, redaction, Privacy Act amendment, retention disposition, legal interpretation, adjudication, enforcement, procurement, and audit authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated government-and-public-sector public records specialist for records completeness, search evidence, official-record status, retention/access governance, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
